title: Phenomenon 60
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:Default Room}} {{Blurimage|file=Yip.webp|caption=An image of a Default Room.|width=350px}} ==Description== The '''Default Room''', also known as '''Phenomenon 60''', is an anomalous location or phenomenon that occurs when new levels are characterised by instability, causing cube-shaped rooms to glitch out and be replaced by [[The Void]] or the '''Default Room'''. However, these rooms are temporary and dissipate within a few minutes, replaced by the level's intended design. In rarer cases, one can be transported into the room directly when [[Noclipping|noclipping]] to a new level; however, they will teleport into the intended level minutes later. The '''Default Room''''s appearance is minimal: an indestructible white drywall room encompassing a fireplace in a corner - the chimney blocked off, and a locked glass door with a blindingly bright light. As a phenomenon, one of the walls will demanifest to serve as an entrance or exit. Additional features, such as the arbitrarily functional outlets and a nylon beige carpet, are the only things that are destructible. The '''Default Room''' does not exhibit the properties nor effects of the level it is in. As a result, a default room could serve as a temporary resting area in unsafe conditions, although entities can still barge in. The '''Default Room''''s singular properties are minimal. Hydration and starvation are mostly void, if not absent; the carpet is unable to absorb liquid, and noclipping is not possible. It is theorised that the '''Default Room''' had been a popular level in the past, with past (~2005) reports describing a somewhat similar, though complex description of it more akin to that of a level. Nonetheless, after reports died down then rose again in 2007, further descriptions described the present-day appearance of the '''Default Room'''. This is yet a theory as past reports are unreliable due to their vagueness, and the fact attributed writers do not remember writing such a report. Such an event has been previously observed in the [[Level 130|old Level 13]], wherein wanderers had their memories forgotten or replaced, attributed to the level. It is unknown if it is truly a standalone location. Due to the '''Default Room''''s temporary nature akin to a phenomenon, only being replaced with the actual level in minutes, and as a standalone location which teleports you to the intended level within minutes, it is unknown if this standalone location is outside the level or is a replacement of the level.
> The grandiose art of erosion;
> The thing that places in the nock-and-crannies of your mind;
> To be now forgotten;
> Existence now simplified;
> An identity overwritten.
